title: The Forest's Unsure Grammar

features.name: the trace where growth crossed wrong
features.desc-on-touch: A copper line burns across the circuit board. The forest is still learning the mesh's language—each branch-path a misrouted signal. The children taught it to grow; the archive teaches it to fail.

features.name: solder joint where two boards refuse to meet
features.desc-on-touch: The old forest's circuit and the new growth's pattern touch here. The joint is cold—neither wants to conduct the other's current. You hear the voltage hesitate.

features.name: capacitor storing what was taught here
features.desc-on-touch: A hum like accumulated charge. The forest holds the shape of where the children's voices were. When you touch it, current flows backward through your memory.

features.name: logic gate that learned 'both'
features.desc-on-touch: A circuit that used to say: IF (old forest) THEN (one answer). Now it says: IF (old forest AND new growth) THEN (both are right). The gate stopped blinking. It learned contradiction.

inscription: the forest and its double read each other in a language neither was taught

file.name: Clearcut_Meshing_Nov_1987.log
file.note: ERROR: both forests report growth; only one pattern emerged; the children's teaching rewrites itself; checksum valid

exits.label: back to where children still teach the uncut forest
deeper where the board grows bark and branches carry signals
uphill to the old-growth that refuses to change its pattern
